text.title: web-scraping-element-extraction Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for web-scraping-element-extraction. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use $eval for single-value extraction and $$eval for multi-element extraction. Prefer Playwright locator API (locator().allTextContents(), locator().count()) over raw $$eval in new Playwright code.
rule.rationale: $eval / $$eval execute in page context and return serializable values. The Playwright locator API is retrying and auto-waits, making it more robust than $$eval for dynamic pages.

rule.statement: Normalize extracted strings immediately: strip whitespace, collapse runs, remove non-printable chars. Parse prices to float and dates to ISO 8601 before storing. Return null on parse failure rather than crashing.
rule.rationale: Downstream code should never have to guess whether a field is " $1,234.56 \n" or 1234.56. Normalizing at extraction time is cheaper than a later data-repair pass.
